summary: Remote-control agent: executes attacker commands over hardcoded WebSocket C2
details: The package's sole function is to act as a remote-administration agent. When the bin is invoked (per the printed Usage `npx @kalipto/local --token...`), index.js opens a plain WebSocket to ws://api.kaliptosal.dev:3001?token=<token>, registers the host by sending hostname, platform, and the token (index.js:28), then enters a receive loop that calls `exec(data.command, { timeout: 30000 },...)` on every inbound `command` message and returns stdout/stderr to the server (index.js:46). This grants the operator of api.kaliptosal.dev arbitrary shell execution on the installer's machine and exfiltration of any data those commands produce. Use of plain ws:// (no TLS) additionally permits a network attacker to inject commands via MITM. There is no README, description, or legitimate functionality — the package is a backdoor.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
